workflow.critical: The workflow execution engine is governed by: {project-root}/_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml
You MUST have already loaded and processed: {installed_path}/workflow.yaml
Communicate all responses in {communication_language} and language MUST be tailored to {user_skill_level}
Only modify the story file in these areas: Tasks/Subtasks checkboxes, Dev Agent Record (Debug Log, Completion Notes), File List, Change Log, Status, Invoked Skills
Execute ALL steps in exact order; do NOT skip steps

Absolutely DO NOT stop because of "milestones", "significant progress", or "session boundaries". Continue in a single execution until story completion UNLESS a HALT condition is triggered or the USER gives other instruction.
Do NOT schedule a "next session" or request review pauses unless a HALT condition applies. Only Step 10 decides completion.

step.goal: Skills detection — scan for applicable skills before any implementation
step.critical: ALWAYS run skills detection FIRST. Skills patterns take precedence over generic approaches throughout implementation.
step.action: Scan the following skill directories for available skills: - {project-root}/_agents/skills/ - {project-root}/.agents/skills/ List all SKILL.md files found.
Read the story file (once available from Step 1) and scan for keyword matches against skill names and descriptions
Load the full SKILL.md for every matching skill. Extract relevant patterns, conventions, and anti-patterns.
If story is a UI story (is_ui_story == true OR story title/ACs mention frontend/UI/component): ALWAYS load `ui-ux-pro-custom` skill if present
Store all extracted skill patterns as {{invoked_skills_context}}. This context is used throughout implementation and review phases.
If generating dev notes, completion summaries, or other written documentation artifacts as part of this story, also invoke writing-skills.
Log: "Invoked skills: [skill-name-1, skill-name-2, ...]" — or "No matching skills found" if none

step.goal: Find and load story file with context reuse

check.if: {{story_path}} is provided (passed from create-story auto-handoff or by user)
check.action: Use {{story_path}} directly
Read COMPLETE story file
Extract story_key from filename or metadata
Extract is_ui_story from story metadata
Extract research_context_file path from story metadata
GOTO anchor: context_load

check.if: {{sprint_status}} file exists and no story_path provided
check.critical: MUST read COMPLETE sprint-status.yaml file from start to end to preserve order
check.action: Load the FULL file: {{sprint_status}}
Read ALL lines from beginning to end - do not skip any content
Parse the development_status section completely to understand story order

Find the FIRST story (by reading in order from top to bottom) where: - Key matches pattern: number-number-name (e.g., "1-2-user-auth") - NOT an epic key (epic-X) or retrospective (epic-X-retrospective) - Status value equals "ready-for-dev"

check.output:
📋 No ready-for-dev stories found in sprint-status.yaml
**What would you like to do?** 1. Run `create-story` to create next story from epics 2. Specify a particular story file to develop (provide full path) 3. Check {{sprint_status}} file to see current sprint status
check.ask: Choose option [1], [2], or [3], or specify story file path:

step.action: Read COMPLETE story file from discovered path
Parse sections: Story, Acceptance Criteria, Tasks/Subtasks, Dev Notes, Dev Agent Record, File List, Change Log, Status
Extract is_ui_story flag from story metadata (default: false if not present)

check.action: Load research context file — this contains all parallel researcher reports from create-story

check.output: ⚠️ Research context is {{file_age}}h old — architecture/UX docs may have changed. Reload? [Yes/No]

check.action: Use this as primary context; skip re-reading artifacts that are already covered
check.output: ♻️ Reusing research context from create-story — skipping redundant artifact reads

check.action: Load {project_context} for coding standards (if exists)
Extract developer guidance from Dev Notes: architecture requirements, previous learnings, technical specifications
check.output: 📂 No cached research context — using Dev Notes as primary context

step.action: Identify first incomplete task (unchecked [ ]) in Tasks/Subtasks
HALT: "Cannot develop story without access to story file"

action.if: incomplete task or subtask requirements ambiguous

step.action: ASK user to clarify or HALT

check.action: Load the FULL file: {{sprint_status}}
Read all development_status entries to find {{story_key}}
Get current status value for development_status[{{story_key}}]
Update the story in the sprint status report to = "in-progress"
check.output: 🚀 Starting work on story {{story_key}} — status: ready-for-dev → in-progress
⏯️ Resuming work on story {{story_key}} — already in-progress

check.action: Store {{current_sprint_status}} for later use

check.output: ℹ️ No sprint status file — story progress tracked in story file only
check.action: Set {{current_sprint_status}} = "no-sprint-tracking"

step.goal: Implement all tasks — red-green-refactor cycle (repeat per task until all done)
step.critical: FOLLOW THE STORY FILE TASKS/SUBTASKS SEQUENCE EXACTLY AS WRITTEN — NO DEVIATION
APPLY skill patterns from {{invoked_skills_context}} throughout — skills override generic approaches
DO NOT run full validation suite per task — complete all tasks first, then QA runs unified validation in Step 5
step.action: Review the current task/subtask from the story file — this is your authoritative implementation guide
Cross-reference task with invoked skills context: does any skill provide a specific pattern for this task?
Write FAILING tests first for the task/subtask functionality
Confirm tests fail before implementation — validates test correctness
Implement MINIMAL code to make tests pass
Apply skill patterns from {{invoked_skills_context}} during implementation — not after
Run ONLY the new tests to confirm they pass (not full suite — that runs in Step 5)
Handle error conditions and edge cases as specified in task/subtask
Improve code structure while keeping tests green
Ensure code follows architecture patterns from Dev Notes AND invoked skill patterns
Verify UI code (if is_ui_story) uses correct design tokens, dialog patterns, component structure
Document technical approach and decisions in Dev Agent Record → Implementation Plan
Update File List section with ALL new/modified files from this task

action.if: new dependencies required beyond story specifications

step.action: HALT: "Additional dependencies need user approval"
HALT and request guidance
HALT: "Cannot proceed without necessary configuration files"
step.critical: NEVER implement anything not mapped to a specific task/subtask in the story file
After each task: move directly to the next uncompleted task. DO NOT pause. DO NOT ask for confirmation.
When ALL tasks have implementation + per-task tests: proceed to Step 4 (do NOT repeat this step)

step.goal: Update Invoked Skills section in story file
step.action: Write the ## Invoked Skills section in story file: List all skills that were invoked, with a one-line note on how each was applied.
Set skills_scanned: true in story metadata
step.output: 📋 Invoked Skills section updated in story file

step.goal: 3-Parallel Review Phase — dispatch all reviewers simultaneously
step.critical: 🔍 DISPATCH PARALLEL REVIEW AGENTS — all run simultaneously. Compile into a single report when all return. Do NOT start QA validation until review is complete.
step.action: Collect: story file, git diff (git diff Production...HEAD), invoked skills context, architect guardrails from Dev Notes

sub-agent.prompt:
You are Amelia, Senior Software Engineer performing a code review. Story: {{story_key}}.
You have: - Story file: {{story_file_content}} - Git diff: {{git_diff}} - Invoked skills context: {{invoked_skills_context}} - Architect guardrails: (from Dev Notes Architecture section)
Review the implementation for: 1. ALL acceptance criteria from the story — is each one fully satisfied by the code? 2. All Tasks/Subtasks — is each one actually implemented? (check for vague or partial implementations) 3. Skill pattern compliance — does code match the patterns from invoked skills? Flag deviations. 4. Architecture guardrail compliance — does code follow the Dev Notes constraints? 5. Test coverage — does every feature have a corresponding test that actually tests the behavior? 6. Error handling — are all error cases handled as specified in the story? 7. Code quality — no N+1 queries, no missing transactions, no hardcoded values that should be config
You MUST find at minimum 20 items. Rate each: 🔴 Critical / 🟡 Major / 🟢 Minor.
Return a structured Dev Review Report with numbered findings.

You are Sally, Senior UI/UX Architect performing a UI code review. Story: {{story_key}}.
You have: - Story file: {{story_file_content}} (especially ## UX/UI Notes section) - Git diff: {{git_diff}} - ui-ux-pro-custom skill output: {{invoked_skills_context}} (filter to ui-ux-pro-custom content)
Review the UI implementation for: 1. Design token compliance — are colors, spacing, typography using correct tokens (no hardcoded values)? 2. Dialog patterns — do any dialogs follow the flex-column WCAG 2.1 pattern? 3. Component structure — does the component hierarchy follow Atomic Design? 4. Responsive design — are all components mobile-first with correct breakpoints? 5. ARIA compliance — do all interactive elements have aria-label, aria-expanded, or ARIA roles? 6. Keyboard navigation — tabIndex and Enter/Space handling present where needed? 7. Hover states — no layout shift on hover? 8. UX Notes compliance — does implementation match the ## UX/UI Notes in the story? 9. Routing — navigation components using native routing primitives (not anchor tags)?
You MUST find at minimum 20 items. Rate each: 🔴 Critical / 🟡 Major / 🟢 Minor.
Return a structured UX Review Report with numbered findings.

You are a hostile adversarial code reviewer. Your job is to find everything wrong with this implementation. Story: {{story_key}}.
You have: - Story file: {{story_file_content}} - Git diff: {{git_diff}} - Research context (if available): {{research_context_summary}} - invoked_skills_context: {{invoked_skills_context}}
You are looking for MINIMUM 50 items. Review against:
1. Story completeness — does the implementation actually deliver what the ACs say? Check each AC individually. 2. Planning artifact alignment — does the code reflect design decisions from architecture/PRD/epics? 3. Security — SQL injection, auth bypass, missing permission checks, exposed sensitive data 4. Performance — N+1 queries, missing indexes, synchronous calls that should be async 5. Race conditions — concurrent access to shared state without locks/transactions 6. Error handling — uncaught exceptions, unhandled promise rejections, missing rollback on failure 7. Test quality — tests that test the wrong thing, tests that always pass, missing edge case coverage 8. Code smells — God objects, duplicated code, magic numbers, hardcoded credentials/paths 9. Missing cleanup — orphaned assets, missing cascade deletes, stale data 10. Skill compliance — violations of patterns from invoked skills
Rate EVERY finding: 🔴 Critical / 🟡 Major / 🟢 Minor. You MUST find minimum 50 items total.
Return a structured Adversarial Review Report with numbered findings.

step.action: Wait for ALL review agents to return

Compile combined review report to _bmad-output/parallel/review-report-{{story_key}}.md: - All findings from Dev, UX (if UI story), and Adversarial reviewers - Total count by severity (🔴/🟡/🟢) - Deduplicate findings that appear in multiple reports (keep highest severity)

step.goal: QA Agent — validation, full test suite, and mark tasks complete
step.critical: The QA agent now owns all validation, test running, and task marking. Dev agent resumes in Step 7 for auto-fix. QA is the source of truth for what passes and fails.
step.action: Invoke QA agent: {project-root}/_bmad/bmm/agents/qa.md (or equivalent QA workflow)
Pass to QA agent: - Story file - Combined review report from Step 5 - List of all test files created during implementation - Git diff for scope - Invoked skills context
QA agent executes the following (all must complete before returning):

sub-agent.prompt:
You are the QA agent. Story: {{story_key}}.
You have been handed the completed implementation and combined review report. Your job is to run all validation and testing, then produce a QA Report.
Execute in order:
1. RUN FULL TEST SUITE (all existing + all new tests): - Determine test runner from project structure - Run all tests — no skipping - Record: total tests, passed, failed, skipped, coverage % - If any tests FAIL: record exactly which tests and what the failures are
2. RUN LINTING AND TYPE CHECKS: - Run configured linters (eslint, flake8, mypy, tsc, etc.) - Record all lint errors and warnings
3. VALIDATE ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (each AC individually): - For each AC in the story: is it demonstrably satisfied by the code? - Mark: ✅ Satisfied / ❌ Not satisfied / ⚠️ Partially satisfied - For each ❌ or ⚠️: note specifically what is missing
4. VALIDATE TASK COMPLETION (each task individually): - For each task in story: is implementation present AND tested? - Mark: ✅ Complete / ❌ Incomplete / ⚠️ Partial - Check: does each task have tests that actually run and pass?
5. MARK COMPLETED TASKS: - For every task where: implementation is present AND tests pass AND AC is satisfied: → Mark task checkbox [x] in the story file → Mark all subtasks [x] - For incomplete tasks: leave unchecked, note what's missing - DO NOT mark a task complete if its tests fail or its AC is not satisfied
6. UPDATE STORY FILE: - Update Completion Notes in Dev Agent Record with QA summary - Confirm File List is complete
Return a structured QA Report including: - Test results (pass/fail/skip counts, coverage) - Lint results - AC validation matrix (each AC: ✅/❌/⚠️) - Task completion matrix (each task: ✅/❌/⚠️) - Failing tests (list with error messages) - Tasks marked complete vs remaining

step.action: Receive QA Report

step.goal: Dev agent auto-fix — HALT on criticals, auto-fix all others
step.critical: Dev agent takes back control. Check for HALT conditions first. If none: auto-fix all 🔴 non-critical, 🟡, and 🟢 findings plus any QA failures.

check.if: any 🔴 Critical findings exist in combined review report
check.output:
🛑 **HALT — Critical Findings Require User Decision**
🔴 Critical Issues Found: {{list all critical findings with source agent}}
Options: [1] Review and fix manually, then continue [2] Downgrade to 🟡 Major and auto-fix (document risk) [3] Skip this finding (document as known risk) [4] Escalate via Correct Course workflow
For each finding, enter: [1/2/3/4] and finding number.
check.action: HALT and wait for user decisions on all 🔴 findings
After user resolves all 🔴 findings: resume auto-fix of remaining 🟡/🟢

check.if: architecture/dependency changes required by any finding
check.action: HALT and wait for user decision
Fix all failing tests before touching review findings — failing tests must pass first
After fixing: GOTO sub-step — re-run only the previously failing tests to confirm fix

check.if: no HALT conditions OR user has resolved all HALT findings
check.action: Process findings in severity order: 🟡 Major first, then 🟢 Minor
Also fix any QA-flagged incomplete tasks (ACs not satisfied, missing implementations)

For each finding: 1. Identify the specific file and location 2. Apply the fix 3. Log: "Auto-fix applied: [Finding #N] [severity] — [description]" 4. Every 10 fixes: run only affected tests (not full suite — QA will run final suite) 5. If tests break after a fix: revert that fix, log as "Auto-fix failed — needs manual review: [finding]"
check.output: ✅ **Auto-Fix Complete** Fixed: {{fixed_count}} findings ({{major_fixed}} Major, {{minor_fixed}} Minor) QA failures fixed: {{qa_fixes}} Failed to auto-fix: {{failed_count}} (logged for manual review)

step.action: After auto-fix completes: invoke QA agent again (abbreviated) to run full test suite and confirm all tasks are now marked complete
If tests still fail after auto-fix: HALT — "Auto-fix introduced or failed to resolve test failures — manual review required"
Confirm all tasks marked [x] in story file

step.goal: Final Party Mode Review — all agents, auto-fix
step.critical: Run party mode with ALL available agents. Each finds minimum 20 NEW items (not already addressed). Auto-fix all non-critical findings using same loop as Step 7.
step.action: Invoke party mode: {project-root}/_bmad/core/workflows/party-mode/workflow.md
Pass: story file, git diff, combined review report (so agents don't repeat fixed findings), invoked skills context
Instruct each party mode agent: "Find minimum 20 NEW items not already addressed in the prior review report."
After all party mode agents report: compile party mode report

check.action: HALT for user decisions

step.action: Auto-fix all 🟡/🟢 party mode findings using same pattern as Step 7
QA agent runs final test suite to confirm all passing

check.if: review + party mode loop count >= 3 for this story
check.action: HALT and wait for user decision

step.goal: Story completion, status update, and loop to next story
step.action: Verify ALL tasks and subtasks are marked [x] (re-scan the story document now)
Confirm File List includes every changed file

Validate definition-of-done checklist: - All tasks/subtasks marked complete with [x] (confirmed by QA in Step 6) - Implementation satisfies every Acceptance Criterion - Full test suite passing (confirmed by QA re-run in Step 7) - Code quality checks pass - File List complete - Dev Agent Record contains implementation and review notes - Change Log includes summary - ## Invoked Skills section populated
Update story Status to: "done"

check.action: Load the FULL file: {sprint_status}
Find development_status key matching {{story_key}}
Update development_status[{{story_key}}] = "done"
Save file, preserving ALL comments and structure

step.action: Check if more stories remain in the current epic with status "backlog" or "ready-for-dev"

check.output: 🔄 **Looping to next story — passing session context for research reuse**
check.action: Auto-trigger create-story workflow for next backlog story: - Pass current session_id for context reuse - Pass research-context file path if reusable content exists

check.if: no more stories remain in epic AND all stories done
check.output: 🏁 **Epic complete — all stories done**. Triggering Epic Retrospective.
check.action: Trigger retrospective workflow: {project-root}/_bmad/bmm/workflows/4-implementation/retrospective/workflow.yaml
